behaviors.description: Keyword-frequency analyzer assigns correct sentiment for reviews containing only configured positive/negative words
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is entirely within the keyword-frequency analyzer's scoring logic — comparing word counts against thresholds. No module handoff is involved; this is a pure algorithmic boundary. REAL-INFRA: No OS, network, or subprocess involvement. MOCK-HIDE: No external dependencies to mock — the analyzer takes a string and a config, returns a score. LLM-DEP: Keyword frequency counting is fully deterministic. MULTI-STEP: No. EVIDENCE-LEVEL=Unit, OUTPUT-LEVEL=Unit, no override.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the keyword-frequency analyzer with a known word list config. Pass a review string composed exclusively of words from the positive list, then exclusively negative, then an empty review. Assert returned sentiment matches expected label for each case. No mocking needed.

behaviors.description: Explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review rather than generic boilerplate
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is in output quality — whether the generated explanation quotes or reflects phrases from the input rather than canned language. This cannot be detected at a single function boundary because the failure is in what the analyzer actually produces. LLM-DEP: The description states the explanation analyzer 'interprets the review in context and produces natural language reasoning,' which is characteristic of LLM generation. A deterministic stub would return a fixed string; you could make any stub pass trivially by including the phrase. Running 100 times with a real model could yield varying phrasings. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking would always pass — only a real model call can reveal whether the model anchors to input text vs. boilerplate. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (b) Correctness depends on real AI/ML output quality → Agentic. REJECTION-GATE: No text in the behavior description disqualifies Agentic; 'natural language reasoning' cannot be deterministically stubbed to expose this failure. EVIDENCE-LEVEL=Agentic, OUTPUT-LEVEL=Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a review with distinctive, uncommon phrases (e.g., 'the latch mechanism is oddly satisfying') to the real explanation analyzer. Assert that the returned explanation contains one or more verbatim or clearly paraphrased tokens from the input, and does not match a known boilerplate template. Run with multiple distinct reviews to check that explanations differ in content.

behaviors.description: Sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule when a review contains mixed positive and negative keywords
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The tie-breaking rule is a configurable threshold decision inside the keyword-frequency analyzer. The failure appears within a single function's branching logic — it does not require any module handoff. REAL-INFRA: No. LLM-DEP: Tie-breaking is deterministic arithmetic against configured thresholds. MOCK-HIDE: No dependencies to hide. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No real OS/network. (b) No AI/ML output dependence. (c) No multi-step chain. EVIDENCE-LEVEL=Unit, OUTPUT-LEVEL=Unit, no override.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize the keyword-frequency analyzer with multiple tie-breaking configs (e.g., 'prefer_negative', 'prefer_positive', 'neutral_on_tie'). Pass reviews with equal positive and negative keyword counts. Assert returned sentiment matches each config's specified tie-breaking rule. All inputs are controlled strings; no mocking.

behaviors.description: Explanation analyzer correctly identifies negative intent in a sarcastic review despite positive surface words
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Sarcasm detection requires pragmatic interpretation of natural language — the failure is in whether the model understands implied negative intent, not in any module-wiring boundary. LLM-DEP: Sarcasm identification from surface-positive text ('Great, another broken feature') is an AI/ML quality problem. A deterministic stub cannot reproduce this failure; you would stub it to pass. Running 100 times with a real model exposes whether it reliably resolves irony. MOCK-HIDE: Any stub returning 'negative' would always pass; only the real model call reveals whether sarcasm detection actually works. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (b) Correctness depends on real AI/ML pragmatic reasoning → Agentic. REJECTION-GATE: The behavior names 'correctly identifies negative intent despite positive surface words' — no text in the description disqualifies Agentic. EVIDENCE-LEVEL=Agentic, OUTPUT-LEVEL=Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated set of sarcastic reviews (e.g., 'Oh fantastic, it broke again on day one') alongside genuinely positive controls to the real explanation analyzer. Assert that sarcastic reviews produce explanations identifying negative or ironic intent, and that positive controls produce genuinely positive explanations. Accept some statistical tolerance across runs if needed.

behaviors.description: Both score and explanation are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure has two parts: (a) structural — both fields present in the response, and (b) semantic consistency — the explanation must align in sentiment direction with the score. Part (a) is an integration concern, but part (b) requires the LLM to actually produce output that agrees with the deterministic score. LLM-DEP: The consistency requirement ('explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score') cannot be validated with a stub — a stub always returns whatever you hardcode. Only a real LLM call reveals whether the explanation independently produces a conflicting polarity. MOCK-HIDE: Stubbing the explanation to return 'this is negative' would trivially satisfy alignment; only a real model call can expose a real mismatch. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (b) Correctness of sentiment-direction alignment in a natural-language explanation depends on real AI/ML output → Agentic. MULTI-STEP: Only one agentic step in the pipeline; the keyword analyzer is deterministic, so this is Agentic not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: 'explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score' cannot be exercised without a real model call — no text disqualifies Agentic. EVIDENCE-LEVEL=Agentic, OUTPUT-LEVEL=Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a set of clearly positive and clearly negative reviews through the full service pipeline using the real explanation analyzer. Assert the response object contains both 'score' and 'explanation' fields. For each response, parse the explanation for explicit sentiment-polarity tokens (e.g., 'positive', 'negative', 'disappointed', 'pleased') and assert their dominant polarity matches the returned score label.
